title: Shadowing in Rust
content:
### Shadowing in Rust

Shadowing allows declaring a new variable with the same name as a previous variable, effectively hiding the original. This is different from mutation.

---

### Shadowing vs Mutation

| Aspect | Shadowing | Mutation |
|--------|-----------|----------|
| Syntax | `let x = ...` again | `let mut x = ...; x = ...` |
| Type change | Allowed | Not allowed |
| Mutability change | Allowed | Not applicable |
| New memory | Creates a new binding | Modifies existing binding |

---

### Examples

```rust
let x = 5;
let x = x + 1;  // shadows, now x = 6

// Type change via shadowing
let spaces = "   ";       // &str
let spaces = spaces.len(); // usize (type changed!)

// Scope-based shadowing
let y = 10;
{
    let y = 20;  // shadows outer y inside this block
    println!("{}", y);  // 20
}
println!("{}", y);  // 10 (outer y restored)
```

> **Key Point:** Shadowing allows you to reuse a variable name after performing transformations, including changing its type. This can improve code clarity by avoiding the need for separate variable names like `input`, `input_trimmed`, `input_parsed`.

quiz.question: Can shadowing change the type of a variable?
options: Yes, because it creates a new binding
